variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
variants.expected_answer: From the construction, $MN$ is the perpendicular bisector of $BC$, so $DB = DC$. Therefore, the perimeter of $\triangle ACD$ is $AC + AD + CD = AC + AD + BD = AC + AB = 5 + 12 = \boxed{17}$.

variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $AD$ is the median of $ \triangle ABC$, $CE$ is the median of $ \triangle ACD$. If the area of $ \triangle ABC$ is $12\text{cm}^2$, what is the area of $ \triangle CDE$?
variants.expected_answer:
\textbf{Solution:} Since $AD$ is the median of side $BC$ of $\triangle ABC$, and the area of $\triangle ABD$ is $12\text{cm}^2$,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle ADC$ is: $\frac{1}{2}\times 12=6\text{cm}^2$,\\
Since $CE$ is the median of side $AD$ of $\triangle ACD$,\\
therefore, the area of $\triangle CDE$ is: $\frac{1}{2}\times 6=\boxed{3}\text{cm}^2$

variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, $OP$ bisects $\angle AOB$. Point $E$ is on $OA$ with $OE = 4$, and the distance from point $P$ to $OB$ is $2$. What is the area of $\triangle POE$?
variants.expected_answer: Since $OP$ bisects $\angle AOB$ and the distance from point $P$ to $OB$ is $2$, the distance from point $P$ to $OA$ is also $2$. Therefore, $S_{\triangle OEP} = \frac{1}{2} \times 4 \times 2 = 4$.
